heading: Oaths
label: 7105 Oaths
text: An applicant for a license or certificate of registry shall take, before the issuance of the license or certificate, an oath, without concealment or reservation, that the applicant will perform faithfully and honestly, according to the best skill and judgment of the applicant, all the duties required by law.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)710546:229e46:23146:244Section 7105 requires all individuals who wish to be issued a license or certificate of registry to take an oath before a government official that they will perform all the duties required by law according to their best skill and judgment.
Amendments2010— struck out “before a designated official” after “an oath”.Pub. L. 111–281
amendments: Amendments2010— struck out “before a designated official” after “an oath”.Pub. L. 111–281
